message:
fix(fcp-webhook): make stripe compliance vector use runtime timestamp (br-pmosg)

crates/fcp-webhook/tests/webhook_receiver_compliance.rs:129-175 (commit
08dcdf06) — stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads
hard-coded t=1_700_000_000 (Nov 14 2023) and a pre-computed HMAC while
verify_and_parse reads Utc::now() with a 5-second tolerance. The test
has been broken since landing (TimestampValidation: 1700000000 vs
current_time 1776967230, tolerance 5s).

Fix: compute the timestamp and HMAC at test time from Utc::now().
The compliance contract this test asserts (signature verifies,
tamper fails, HMAC matches the recomputed value) is preserved; the
byte-stable "known-good vector" property relaxes to structural
stability, because StripeWebhook exposes no public test-clock override
(with_fixed_now_for_tests is #[cfg(test)] and crate-private).

Verified:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline -p fcp-webhook \
    --test webhook_receiver_compliance
  -> running 6 tests; 6 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured.

Closes flywheel_connectors-pmosg.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
